title: VPN Searches Spike After Telegram Ban In India
content: Until June 15, search interest was at zero, indicating no notable demand. But on June 16, as the restrictions began, the numbers started rising rapidly from 0 to 5, then 18, 28, and peaking at 99 within hours.

title: Kerala Woman, 75, Dies After Falling From Scooter While Escaping Stray Dogs
content: The victim, identified as Suhra, a resident of Muttichur, suffered a severe head injury after falling from the two-wheeler near the Kallattupuzha Siva Temple around 12.30 pm on Tuesday.

title: "New Momentum, New Energy In Ties": PM Modi's Top Quotes At Trump Meeting
content: PM Modi met US President Donald Trump on the sidelines of the G7 Summit in France, with the two leaders discussing India-US ties, peace efforts in West Asia, and the safety of Indian sailors amid tensions around the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Opinion: Public Assets, Private Capital: India's Next Reform Step
content: That gap between what India's sovereign assets are worth and what they currently earn is central to India's location in the low-middle income bracket.

title: G7 Summit 2026 LIVE Updates: PM Modi, Trump Hold Bilateral Meet
content: G7 Summit 2026 LIVE Updates: This is India's 13th participation in the G7 as a guest nation and PM Modi's seventh consecutive appearance at the summit.

title: "Calm, Cool And A Total Killer": Trump's Big Praise For PM Modi At G7 Meet
content: "Unlike PM Modi, who's calm, cool and a total killer, I am not. Look at him," Trump said as the two leaders prepare for talks on the sidelines of the G7 Summit.

title: India, European Union Push To Seal Landmark Trade Pact By Year-End
content: The renewed momentum emerged during a meeting between PM Modi, European Council President Antonio Costa and European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen on the sidelines of the G7 Summit in Evian, France.

title: Trump Halts Intelligence Chief Confirmation, Renews Vote Curb Demand
content: US President Donald Trump said Wednesday he had suspended a Senate hearing on his own nominee for intelligence chief, in a fresh effort to push Congress to back his bill on voting restrictions.

title: J&K Police Busts Cross-Border Narco-Terror Module, 3 Arrested With Weapons
content: Police recovered an AK-47 rifle, a pistol, and 500 grams of narcotics allegedly dropped by drones from Pakistan.

title: Video: At G7 Summit, Trump Declares "I'm The Boss", Draws Laughter
content: The G7 summit of world powers in France is being chaired by President Emmanuel Macron as host but on Wednesday his guest US President Donald Trump left no doubt over who he believed was in charge.

title: Weather Today Live Updates: Delhi Records Coolest Day Of June As Rain Brings Respite From Heat
content: Weather Today, Delhi-NCR Rain Alert Live Updates: Delhi NCR experiences cooler weather due to rain and thunderstorm

title: Essel Group's Subhash Chandra Sells Delhi Bungalow For Rs 1,260 Crore
content: Essel Group Chairman Subhash Chandra has sold a prime bungalow in Delhi's prestigious Lutyens' zone for Rs 1,260 crore, in what is being described as one of the most expensive property transactions in recent years, sources told news ageny PTI.

title: "Targeted, Tied To NEET Re-exam Window": Centre Defends Telegram Ban
content: Officials stated the ban was a preemptive move to prevent organised fraud and extortion targeting students, rather than an attempt to suppress discussion around paper leaks.

title: Starbucks To Shut 2,000 South Korea Stores For Half A Day After Promo Row
content: Coffee chain to hold 'historical awareness' training after backlash against marketing campaign that evoked 1980 military crackdown.

title: PM Modi, German Chancellor Merz Review Strategic Partnership At G7 Summit
content: During the talks, the two leaders reviewed progress in ongoing bilateral initiatives and expressed satisfaction over the renewed momentum in relations following Chancellor Merz's recent visit to India.

title: Dhurandhar Shows How Counter-Terrorism Ops Should Be Conducted: Israeli Envoy
content: According to Azar, one of the film's strongest aspects is its depiction of the interconnected nature of terrorism, terror financing, drug trafficking and counterfeit currency operations.

title: 'It's a rough profession': Trump on death of 3 Indian seafarers during meeting with PM Modi
content: Prime Minister Modi and US President Trump met at the G7 Summit, prioritizing maritime security and the safety of Indian seafarers. Modi stressed the vital importance of open shipping routes, particularly the Strait of Hormuz, and commended Trump's efforts in West Asia. The leaders also discussed trade deals, signaling renewed energy in India-US relations after a period of strain.

title: Age of software export controls starts with a bug report as US treats AI model like a missile
content: The United States has expanded export controls to cover advanced AI models. This means access to powerful AI is now treated like a physical export. Companies like Anthropic must restrict access for foreign nationals. This action signifies a major shift in how technology is regulated. It suggests AI is now considered strategic infrastructure, not just software.

title: Minor girl forced into illegal conversion and marriage; Kutch police arrest Ajmer Dargah Imam
content: Rapar police have arrested an Imam from Ajmer for allegedly facilitating the illegal religious conversion and Nikah of a 17-year-old girl. The girl's family reported her abduction by Jahin Abdul Chauhan, who allegedly assaulted her and forced her into marriage after conversion. Both accused are in police custody.

title: Salesforce CEO Marc Benioff shares selfie with PM Modi at G7 summit: What he said
content: Salesforce CEO Marc Benioff shared a selfie with Prime Minister Narendra Modi at the G7 Summit in France, discussing AI's role in digital governance. Benioff also met other tech leaders like Google DeepMind's CEO, focusing on responsible AI deployment and online safety for minors. PM Modi engaged with various global leaders on diverse issues.

title: Longer than World War I: 10 ways Russia-Ukraine conflict has changed the world for decades to come
content: The Russia-Ukraine war, now longer than WWI, is fundamentally reshaping global order. It has triggered NATO expansion, a European arms race, and elevated drone warfare. Russia faces deep dependency on China, while North Korea and Iran profit as key arms suppliers. Beijing learns from Moscow's failures, and India navigates US pressure over Russian oil.

title: Video of namaz being offered inside Gujarat hospital sparks row
content: A video showing individuals praying inside Vadodara's SSG Hospital OPD has sparked a controversy. Hospital administration is investigating the incident, which reportedly occurred Monday evening. Authorities confirmed the individuals appeared to be patient relatives, and their prayers caused no disruption to services. The VHP and Bajrang Dal have sought clarification from the hospital.

title: Fuel, forex and conflict hit airlines: Domestic carriers’ profits may fall 15% this year
content: Indian airlines anticipate a 10-15% drop in operating profits this fiscal year. Soaring aviation fuel prices, airspace limitations, and a weakening rupee, exacerbated by the Middle East conflict, are significantly impacting the sector. Despite potential fuel price moderation, higher costs and lease rentals will continue to pressure airline finances.

title: Dell CEO Michael Dell makes one of largest public university donations in US history
content: Michael Dell has gifted a staggering $750 million to the University of Texas at Austin, propelling the creation of a groundbreaking AI-native hospital and research campus. This substantial donation, adding to his family's legacy of support, will also bolster scholarships, advanced computing, and vital medical research, underscoring Dell's enduring connection to his alma mater.

title: India-UK FTA to take effect from July 15, UK envoy calls it a 'historic moment'
content: India and the UK will implement their landmark free trade agreement on July 15, significantly reducing tariffs on key products like whisky and automobiles. This historic pact is set to boost bilateral trade by billions and enhance economic cooperation, with businesses having 28 days to prepare for its benefits.

title: After 8,000 layoffs, Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg makes a 'confession' to employees
content: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg admitted in an internal memo that the company "made mistakes" during its AI overhaul, after laying off 8,000 employees and reassigning 7,000 to AI teams in May. He promised no more company-wide layoffs in 2026 but refused to overpromise. With morale at record lows and capex hitting $145 billion, his fixes—a July hackathon, assigned desks, bigger event budgets—drew a chilly response from a workforce that's heard enough reassurances.

title: NFL trade rumors: Bengals, Giants Eye $3M Cardinals Star
content: The Arizona Cardinals’ star player James Conner is being speculated to be traded from the team ahead of the new season, sparking chaos among fans about his future. Though many teams have been linked to him, the Cincinnati Bengals and the New York Giants have emerged as potential landing spots for James Conner.

title: Shreyas Iyer surpasses Virat Kohli, becomes joint second-fastest Indian to...
content: Shreyas Iyer achieved a significant milestone in his ODI career. He became the joint second-fastest Indian batter to reach 3,000 runs. Iyer reached this landmark in his 72nd innings. This places him alongside Shikhar Dhawan and ahead of Virat Kohli. The achievement occurred during India's second ODI against Afghanistan in Lucknow. Shubman Gill remains the fastest Indian to this mark.

title: From Iran missions to California fireball: What we know about fatal B-52 crash
content: A US Air Force B-52H Stratofortress crashed shortly after takeoff at Edwards Air Force Base, killing all eight personnel aboard. The iconic bomber, a symbol of American air power, was on a routine test mission. Aerial footage showed the aircraft bursting into flames, with tracking data indicating a rapid, uncontrolled descent.

title: Meet Antonela Roccuzzo, Lionel Messi’s childhood love
content: Lionel Messi and Antonela Roccuzzo share one of sport's most enduring love stories, rooted in their shared childhood in Rosario, Argentina. They married in July 2017 and have three sons together: Thiago, Mateo, and Ciro. Roccuzzo has built a massive global following in her own right, known less for glamour and more for consistent family presence through every chapter of Messi's extraordinary career. As Messi competes at the 2026 World Cup, their family remains one of the most recognised in football.

title: Pentagon renaming of Pacific command deepens unease as Trump assures 'we are there for India'
content: The Pentagon has reverted the Indo-Pacific Command to its former name, Pacific Command, sparking concerns about India's strategic standing. While officials claim no mission change, analysts interpret the move, coinciding with a less warm Trump-Modi meeting, as a potential recalibration of US Asia policy and diminished enthusiasm for the Quad alliance.

title: 'We are very close to deal': Trump signals progress on India-US trade agreement
content: US President Donald Trump indicated significant progress in trade talks with India, stating a deal is "very close." This follows intensified negotiations and an upcoming visit by the US Trade Representative to finalize a bilateral trade agreement. Most issues are reportedly settled, with discussions focusing on minor details for the initial phase, expected before July 24.

title: Zeenat Aman reveals her Hindu name and why she chose not to follow religion
content: Veteran actress Zeenat Aman revealed her birth name, Laliteshwari, and discussed her upbringing with a Hindu mother and Muslim father. She explained that her diverse cultural exposure led her to embrace humanity, kindness, and love as her religion, rather than adhering to any specific faith.

title: Gill turns up the heat, leaves Virender Sehwag behind to become ...
content: Gill's innings came after India found themselves at 96 for 2. He then joined forces with Ishan Kishan for a 224-run partnership that put India in control of the contest. Both batters went on to score centuries.

title: Prince Yadav's dream debut turns sour as maiden ODI wicket ruled no-ball
content: Debut pacer Prince Yadav experienced a moment of heartbreak as his maiden ODI wicket was disallowed due to a marginal no-ball. Despite the unfortunate turn, Yadav's delivery troubled Afghanistan opener Rahmanullah Gurbaz. This incident occurred after India posted a formidable 402, powered by Shubman Gill's 154 and Ishan Kishan's 125.

title: Now, rebellion in SP? UP minister Rajbhar makes big claim, Akhilesh Yadav responds
content: Samajwadi Party chief Akhilesh Yadav has dismissed claims of a major split within his party. He accused the Bharatiya Janata Party of a history of engineering defections in opposition parties. Yadav stated the Samajwadi Party is prepared for such challenges. These remarks come amid political positioning for the 2027 Uttar Pradesh assembly elections.

title: CJP founder assault case: Out on bail, accused welcomed with garlands
content: Five men accused of assaulting Cockroach Janata Party (CJP) founder Abhijeet Deepke during a Jaipur protest were granted bail Tuesday. The accused, detained for breach of peace, were released on Rs 20,000 personal bonds with six-month restrictions. An argument during the demonstration reportedly escalated into a physical altercation.

title: Hormuz reopening may not ease fertiliser woes anytime soon, say industry executives
content: Fertiliser prices may remain high for another three to four months despite a US-Iran peace deal and the Strait of Hormuz reopening. Industry insiders cite the time needed to restart plants and stabilize raw material supplies, particularly sulphur, which faces ongoing shortages. Lingering shipping disruptions and logistical hurdles will also delay a full return to normal.

title: ITR filing: How to pay zero tax under new and old tax regime - know all about Section 87A rebate
content: Section 87A tax relief to resident individuals whose total income is less than the prescribed limits. How does it work? The tax (before cess) is first calculated according to the applicable tax rates and then reduced by the rebate available under section 87A.

title: 19 trekkers battle avalanche threat to conquer Traill’s Pass; chilling video emerges
content: A 19-member trekking team successfully navigated the treacherous Traill's Pass (5,312m) in the Kumaon Himalayas, overcoming blizzard conditions, deep crevasses, and a near-fatal avalanche. The expedition, described as one of the toughest, demanded exceptional endurance, technical skill, and constant vigilance for safety amidst adverse weather.

title: India A thrash Afghanistan A by 101 runs, storm into Tri-Series final
content: India A secured their spot in the Tri-Nation A Series final with a dominant 101-run win over Afghanistan A. Half-centuries from Arya, Varma, and Kushagra, coupled with Nishant Sindhu's four-wicket haul, propelled India A to a commanding victory and a place in the championship match.

title: Kerala students use cosmetics during breaks; schools draw red line
content: Kerala's Child Welfare Committee has launched a campaign against schoolchildren using cosmetics, citing health risks like intestinal cancer linked to chemicals such as mercury and cadmium.  The initiative, starting in Kollam, aims to educate students and parents about the dangers of these products, with schools completing awareness sessions to be declared 'lipstick-free campuses'

title: Defence production soars to record Rs 1. 78 lakh crore in fiscal 2025-26
content: India's defense production hit an unprecedented Rs 1.78 lakh crore in FY 2025-26, marking a significant 15.6% jump from the previous year. This surge, nearly quadrupling since FY 2013-14, underscores the nation's growing self-reliance and expanding industrial base. The private sector's contribution also reached an all-time high, bolstering defense exports.

title: Sooryavanshi being targeted because others are jealous; 'they will poke him more'
content: Former India batter Manoj Tiwary urged 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi to channel his aggression positively after his altercation with Sri Lanka A players. Tiwary said rival teams would target the youngster because of his rapid rise and advised him to decide the image he wants in cricket. India coach Sairaj Bahutule backed Sooryavanshi, saying the teenager would learn from the episode.

title: Tejas, Akash, BrahMos & more: How India strengthened defence manufacturing over the last decade
content: India's defence production has surged to a record Rs 1.78 lakh crore in FY 2025-26, a significant leap towards self-reliance. Prime Minister Modi highlighted this achievement, showcasing indigenous platforms like Tejas, Akash, Agni-V, and INS Vikrant. This marks a major transformation from import dependence to a capable defence exporter.

title: Economics of buying versus renting a home: What makes sense?
content: India's dream of homeownership faces a reality check in 2026 as property prices and rents surge. Millions of urban Indians are now weighing the financial implications of buying versus renting. While buying offers long-term security, renting might be the smarter move for many amidst rising costs and shrinking affordable housing supply.

title: Ishaan Khatter responds to religion question with message of unity and faith
content: During an Instagram 'Ask Me Anything' session, Ishaan Khatter playfully responded to a fan's question about his religion with a meme referencing multiple faiths. He later clarified his beliefs centered on nature, kindness, humanity, and creation, accompanied by a scenic photo. Khatter's diverse family background includes Muslim and Hindu roots.

title: Kishan eclipses Maxwell's famous Wankhede hundred, hits 71-ball ton to become ...
content: Kishan reached the milestone on the final ball of the 33rd over. He cut a delivery from Bilal Sami through backward point for a boundary to bring up his second ODI century.

title: Mitch Marner highlights mental health after Stanley Cup Final loss
content: The importance of mental health was the focus of Vegas Golden Knights forward Mitch Marner’s first public appearance since the Stanley Cup Final. The NHL star reflected on a tough stretch in his career after a gut-wrenching series loss to the Carolina Hurricanes, but stressed the importance of prioritising mental well-being.

title: Three years on, has Sri Lanka recovered from its economic collapse?
content: Sri Lanka, once a symbol of economic collapse, has achieved remarkable stabilisation. Fuel shortages and soaring inflation have receded, with economic growth returning and tourism booming. While international lenders praise the IMF-backed reforms, significant challenges like high public debt and low investment persist, indicating recovery is still incomplete and transformation is the next crucial step.

title: ISI-linked terror module busted: Delhi Police arrests five more operatives in TTH case
content: Delhi Police's Special Cell has apprehended five more individuals linked to a Pakistan-ISI-backed terror module, Tehreek-e-Taliban Hindustan (TTH). These arrests are part of a wider crackdown on a trans-border network allegedly involved in targeting police, spreading propaganda, and facilitating weapon and drug smuggling from Pakistan.

title: 'I killed her': Bengaluru man calls lawyer after strangling wife
content: Bengaluru police have arrested a water tanker driver, Sharath, accused of strangling his 20-year-old wife, Anusha, following frequent domestic quarrels. The accused allegedly confessed to the crime over a phone call to an advocate before fleeing their Malleswaram residence. Police discovered the victim's body after being alerted by the advocate and subsequently launched a manhunt for Sharath.

title: E100: How India’s new flex fuel norms affect your vehicle and wallet | Business Matters
content: The government has approved rules for 100% ethanol (E100) fuel. What does this mean for motorists? From higher car costs to lower mileage, here’s a simple breakdown of flex-fuel vehicles, costs, and impact on your everyday driving.

title: Women plantation workers from Tamil Nadu joyous about free KSRTC ride
content: But Kumily depot officials say the facility could be restricted to commuters within Kerala’s borders. The average daily collection per bus used to be ₹26,000, but since the implementation of the scheme, it has dropped to below ₹8,000

title: District panchayat draws up plan to address street dog issue
content: Along with the ABC programme, the panchayat has decided to set up more shelters for dogs undergoing sterilisation and make the licensing of pet dogs mandatory

title: 1,000 home guards to be posted in Fire and Rescue Services: Chennithala
content: Action plan to be prepared for comprehensive modernisation of the force against the backdrop of climate change and resultant increase in disasters

title: Let Opposition MPs be guided by their conscience on backing NDA in Parliament: Omar Abdullah
content: We remain committed to our political ideology and will not alter our stand for the sake of power; every party is free to make its own decisions, but the NC’s position is clear, says J&K Chief Minister Omar Abdullah

title: India, Thailand review defence ties, strategic cooperation
content: Defence industry cooperation featured prominently in the talks as India and Thailand explored opportunities to deepen collaboration in defence manufacturing, research, and capability development

title: Kerala CPI to focus on educating cadre in a bid to strengthen itself organisationally
content: The CPI will launch a five-day training programme for teachers of the party school on June 20, followed by classes for its 1.5 lakh members in Kerala, and plans a people’s march to Delhi on September 1 to tackle unemployment and rising prices.

title: India’s defence production hits record ₹1.78 lakh crore in FY 2025-26
content: According to the Defence Ministry, the milestone represents a 15.6% growth over the previous fiscal year’s output of ₹1.54 lakh crore

title: India's education system puts pressure on children, crushes them: Rahul Gandhi in Kota rally
content: The programme “Chhatron ki Goonj” Maha Rally is being projected by the Congress as the first in a series of nationwide student conventions, while the BJP has objected to the outreach, alleging it could distract aspirants preparing for the examination

title: SIT to move court for custody of DYFI leader in ‘kafir screenshot’ case
content: Jithin Bhaskar, a DYFI Vadakara block committee member and admin of the WhatsApp group ‘Vadakara Squad’ is suspected of sharing it with over 200 persons. However, police have not established that he is the creator of the screenshot

title: Watch: Telegram under fire: NTA’s crackdown explained | Above the Fold | 17.06.2026
content: Telegram challenges its temporary NEET block, fresh details emerge on the U.S.-Iran ceasefire deal, and Messi makes World Cup history with a hat-trick - Top stories of the day.

title: War of words erupts in U.P. as BJP ally claims split in Samajwadi Party; Akhilesh hits back
content: State Minister and SBSP president Om Prakash Rajbhar’s statement is echoed by Deputy Chief Minister Keshav Prasad Maurya, who says as many as 26 SP MPs are ready to defect

title: Strike at paper leak mafia, not students: Rahul Gandhi to PM Modi on Telegram curbs
content: Telegram has stopped working for existing users in India after the Centre temporarily restricted access to the messaging app ahead of the June 21 NEET-UG re-examination

title: Lokesh challenges Jagan for a debate on DSC recruitment process
content: Asserting that it has been conducted transparently and in accordance with the horizontal and vertical reservation norms, the HRD Minister accuses the opposition party of spreading misinformation on teacher recruitment, voter enrolment, and welfare initiatives

title: Telugu-only enumeration forms for SIR likely to stir a hornet’s nest
content: The one-language approach may pose difficulties in Hyderabad, one of India’s most linguistically diverse urban centres

title: Delhi High Court issues notice to Centre on Telegram’s challenge to temporary ban ahead of NEET exam
content: During the hearing, Solicitor General Tushar Mehta, who represented the Centre, alleged that several channels on the platform were allegedly being used to offer leaked examination questions in exchange for money

title: Congress aims better performance in future in Thrissur, vows course correction
content: A joint meeting of mandalam and block Congress committee presidents decides to move forward by identifying and rectifying organisational lapses

title: Central portal for Waqf properties offers facility to upload data, says Kerala board
content: Dismissing the allegations that  board had illegally uploaded details of the disputed Munambam property, C.M. Manju, Chief Executive Officer in-charge of the Kerala board, says in her final report that the portal has an enabling provision and technical functionality permitting direct entry of property particulars by the board

title: A.P. launches mandatory disability screening for all students
content: The screening will be conducted through ‘Prashast 2.0’, an upgraded app for screening 21 types of disabilities in children; initiative is being implemented for the academic year 2026-27 as per the directions of the Central Ministry of Education, says Samagra Shiksha State Project Director

title: Shortage of psychiatric social workers grips mental healthcare system in Kerala’s govt. hospitals
content: Eight of the 15 sanctioned posts under the Directorate of Health Services and three of the four posts under the Directorate of Medical Education remain vacant

title: SC seeks Delhi Police response on bail pleas of alleged Indian Mujahideen operatives
content: Top court notes that the January 5 judgment denying bail to Umar Khalid and Sharjeel Imam, which the HC had relied on to reject the accused’s bail pleas, has since been referred to a larger Bench

title: KAT refuses to stay GO that removed  DHS
content: The Kerala government submitted that a new Director of Health Services was appointed, considering the urgent requirements of the State’s health sector

title: Cyber fraudsters are ‘parasites’, must be dealt with firmly, says Supreme Court
content: Top court refuses to entertain a plea by an accused facing multiple cyberfraud cases seeking the clubbing of multiple criminal cases and the grant of bail; society’s interest is there only when you are inside jail and not outside, it says

title: Anit Thapa resigns as Chief Executive of GTA
content: The resignation of Anit Thapa, who was an ally of Trinamool Congress comes a day after West Bengal Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari visited Darjeeling hills and raised the issue of ‘corruption’ in the GTA. After the BJP came to power in West Bengal in May 2026, chairperson of several civic bodies including Kolkata Municipal Corporation and Bidhannagar Municipal Corporation had resigned.

title: Miscreants decamp with ATM, set it ablaze in Anantapur district
content: According to the police, nearly ₹18 lakh cash was stolen from the machine, while a similar theft had taken place in Dharmavaram in neighbouring Sri Sathya Sai district a couple of days ago

title: Kerala to appoint geotechnical experts to examine issues in NH construction
content: According to PWD Minister, 81% of the 642 km project has already been completed. The move is aimed at timely completion of the remaining highway work within a year.

title: Operation Toofan: 36 kg of cannabis seized, two held in Thrissur
content: Kunnamkulam police and DANSAF team intercept accused after dramatic escape bid, officers have a narrow escape as accused try to ram their vehicle into police vehicle

title: Citizens, civic groups eye maintenance push under new Bengaluru Minister
content: Citizens are also expecting better coordination between the Greater Bengaluru Authority and the rural administration to improve connectivity and everyday life

title: Govt. asks employees healthcare trust to finalise guidelines for health scheme
content: EHCT board reconstituted 18 members from the administration including Chief Secretary, special chief secretaries of key departments, CEOs of Rajiv Arogya Sri Trust, SERP and EHS

title: SC defers hearing of plea challenging NEET-UG re-test to July
content: While the re-test is scheduled for June 21, Justice Narasimha’s Bench, before which the matter is to be listed, is scheduled to sit only after July 13, effectively rendering the plea infructuous.

title: Karnataka High Court declares arrest of Gameskraft directors illegal; but allows ED to proceed against them by issuing summons
content: The court found that the ED had arrested them without issuing them summons under Section 50 of the Prevention of Money Launder (PML) Act. Also, the court said that there was no new material to arrest them

title: Candidates without APTET qualification can apply for KGBV posts; deadline extended till June 20
content: Candidates selected through the recruitment process must qualify APTET within one year from the date of joining the service, says Samagra Shiksha Project Director

title: TMC, Sena (UBT) flux: Bid to boost NDA numbers in Lok Sabha for women's bill?
content: govt can bring back its bill to roll out quota for women in Lok Sabha and assemblies from 2029, with nearly 50% rise in seats of legislatures expected in parliament's monsoon session . a top BJP functionary asserts that the bill will be brought in the coming session, a ally says .
